One of the greatest investment bets of all time was investing in AI data centers or compute. Skeptics made two fatal assumptions:

1) They either thought AI would hit a ceiling, or

2) They thought the demand for intelligence would be limited.

Both of these were totally wrong.

At this point, scientific journals have become one of the central chokepoints on scientific progress. Think about how perverse that is: a gatekeeping publishing system standing in the way of discovery, while knowledge that belongs to humanity is locked behind paywalls!

OpenAI should probably bite the bullet and just name their next set of models something more human sounding.

Everyone anthropomorphizes their AIs anyway, and "Claude" is an easier name to refer to than ChatGPT. Also easier to make a gerund, "Clauding," or adjective, "Claudy-y."

An underrated part of AI agents is dropping in a GitHub repo link and saying “make this work” instead of spending 30 minutes trying to follow half-baked onboarding docs and understanding whatever cute key words and processes the creator designed
